questions.question: A startup needs to store 50TB of historical customer data that is infrequently accessed but must be retained for 7 years for compliance. Design an S3-based storage solution including appropriate storage class selection, lifecycle policies, and versioning strategy. Also calculate the approximate monthly cost if 10% of data is accessed monthly.
keyPoints: S3 Intelligent-Tiering or Glacier Deep Archive for cost optimization
Lifecycle policies to transition between storage classes automatically
Enable versioning for compliance and data protection
Consider S3 Replication and Cross-Region Replication for disaster recovery

questions.question: Explain the components of AWS VPC architecture required to deploy a 3-tier web application (Web, Application, Database) with public internet access for the web tier only. Include subnet design, route tables, NAT Gateway, and Security Group configurations with specific port requirements.
keyPoints: Public subnets for ALB/Web servers, Private subnets for App tier, Isolated subnets for RDS
NAT Gateway in public subnet to allow outbound internet for private subnets
Security Groups: Web SG (80/443 from ALB), App SG (80/443 from Web SG), DB SG (3306 from App SG only)
Route tables: Public route via Internet Gateway, Private route via NAT Gateway

questions.question: Design a serverless image processing pipeline on AWS that: (a) accepts image uploads to S3, (b) triggers Lambda to resize images into multiple thumbnail sizes, (c) stores processed images in a separate bucket, (d) uses DynamoDB to store image metadata. Include the Lambda function code (Python), IAM role permissions, S3 event configuration, and explain the pricing model based on 1 million image uploads per month with average size of 2MB.
keyPoints: S3 Event Notification → Lambda trigger with proper IAM role (s3:GetObject, s3:PutObject, dynamodb:PutItem)
Lambda function using Pillow/boto3 for image processing with concurrent execution and reserved concurrency
Architecture: Upload Bucket → Lambda → Processed Bucket + DynamoDB metadata
Pricing: Lambda compute (duration × memory), S3 requests, Data Transfer, DynamoDB write capacity

questions.question: Compare AWS RDS (PostgreSQL), Aurora, and DynamoDB for a high-traffic e-commerce application handling 10,000 transactions per minute with requirements for: (a) ACID compliance for orders, (b) automatic scaling during flash sales, (c) multi-region read replicas, (d) point-in-time recovery. Provide a recommendation with justification and explain how to implement read replicas, backup strategies, and cost optimization using Reserved Instances vs On-Demand.
keyPoints: RDS: Managed PostgreSQL/MySQL with Multi-AZ, read replicas, automatic backups, but limited scaling
Aurora: MySQL/Postgre compatible, auto-scaling storage, 6-way replication, serverless option, 1/10th latency vs RDS
DynamoDB: Fully managed NoSQL, on-demand/Provisioned modes, DAX caching, Global Tables for multi-region
Cost: RDS Reserved Instances (1yr/3yr), Aurora Serverless for variable workload, DynamoDB on-demand for unpredictable traffic
